hero.title:
Extended Warranty
Program
hero.subtitle: Register your system and double your coverage — from 12 to 24 months of manufacturer warranty, at no additional cost.

observation.title: Attention:
observation.description: The power battery is a consumable item and is not covered by the extended warranty program. The warranty also does not cover misuse, negligence, or incorrect installation.

form.title: Activate your extended warranty
form.subtitle: Fill out the form to ensure 24 months of full coverage for your Fert Sensor system.

install-header.title: System installation and setup
install-header.subtitle: — User guide
install-header.description: Follow the steps below to correctly install and configure your Fert Sensor and Monitor AT200.

install-section.title: 1- Installing the Fert Sensor on the implement
install-section.description: Before installing, choose the best position on the implement. Avoid contact between the sensor and metal or moving parts.

steps.description: Secure the sensor with the included Hellermann Anti-UV strap.
Pass the strap through the front channel of the sensor and wrap the hose with the tabs.
Tighten until the sensor is secured without deforming the hose.
Test by shaking the hose — simulate the implement's movement.
Cut off the excess strap after fastening.

observation.title: Note:
observation.description: The sensor is completely wireless and resistant to dust and water — no periodic maintenance is required in the field.

install-section.title: 2- Installing the AT200 Monitor on the tractor
install-section.description: The monitor can be mounted with a suction cup (on glass) or a clamp (on bars). The antenna must always remain vertical.

steps.description: Suction cup: clean the surface, attach it, press firmly, and turn the latch.
Clamp: position the monitor, pass the clamp through, and tighten the screw until secured.
Aim the monitor toward the operator's seat.
Keep the antenna always vertical (range up to 20 m with obstacles).
Avoid placing it behind the cab pillars.

observation.title: Electrical connection:
observation.description: Connect the red cable (3A fuse) to the positive (+) terminal and the black cable to the negative (−) terminal of the tractor battery (12V DC). Avoid contact with moving parts or hot surfaces.

install-section.title: 3- Sensor identification and addressing
install-section.description: Each sensor must be identified with the row number and addressed to the monitor before operation.

steps.description: There are 3 fields on the front of the sensor — use a permanent marker.
Enter the row number (e.g., 001, 002...).
On the monitor, press Menu → Sensor → Address.
Select the row number with ▲▼ and confirm ✓.
Bring the sensor symbol close to the monitor symbol for ~5 seconds.
Wait for confirmation and repeat for each sensor.

observation.title: Note:
observation.description: If the sensor is not recognized, it may be in power-saving mode. Use a screwdriver to tap it lightly and activate it.

install-section.title: 4- Main screen and status reading
install-section.description: On the AT200 main screen, you can monitor the real-time status of all monitored lines.

steps.description: FLOW OK / arrows (›): all lines with normal flow
MOVEMENT: flow interrupted by a stop or maneuver of the implement
FAULT + row number: clogging or lack of material — check the indicated line
LOW BATTERY: the sensor battery needs to be replaced soon
The number in the upper-right corner indicates the lines that are addressed and communicating

observation.title: Electrical connection:
observation.description: Connect the red cable (3A fuse) to the positive (+) terminal and the black cable to the negative (−) terminal of the tractor battery (12V DC). Avoid contact with moving parts or hot surfaces.

install-section.title: 5- Menu settings
install-section.description: The AT200 menu offers brightness, volume, clock, and sensor management adjustments.

steps.description: Brightness/Volume: 5 levels — Menu → Brightness or Volume → ▲▼ → ✓
Clock: Menu → Clock → adjust day, month, year, hour, and minute
Read sensor: Menu → Sensor → Read — check the configured line and monitor
Disable: Menu → Sensor → Disable — removes the line from the network

observation.title: Attention:
observation.description: To cancel any operation, press ✕ — the monitor returns immediately to the main screen.

install-section.title: 6- Replacing the Fert Sensor battery
install-section.description: 3.6V Lithium Battery – 4000mAh, with an approximate lifespan of ~2,500 hours. The monitor warns you in advance when it needs to be replaced.

steps.description: Remove the sensor from the implement — do not replace it while the sensor is installed
Using a Phillips screwdriver, loosen the 4 screws and remove the cover
Pull out the battery and foam to access the connector
Disconnect carefully — do not use excessive force
Connect the new battery (a gentle fit confirms the correct position)
Reinstall the foam, battery, cover, and screws
The sensor automatically resumes communication, keeping the configuration

observation.title: Important:
observation.description: Use original FertiSystem batteries. Damage to wires, connectors, or seals during replacement is not covered by the warranty.

faq-header.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq-header.subtitle: — FAQ
faq-header.description: Choose a category to find your answer quickly.

questions.question: What do I gain by registering my system?
questions.answer: Your warranty is extended from 12 to 24 months at no additional cost. Coverage includes manufacturing defects in the Fert Sensor and the AT200 Monitor, as long as installation, operation, and maintenance follow the instructions in the manual.

questions.question: Do I have a deadline to register?
questions.answer: Yes. Registration must be completed within 30 days after installation. After this period, the benefit may no longer be available.

questions.question: What happens if I do not register?
questions.answer: Your system keeps the standard 12-month warranty from the invoice date. You only lose the additional 12 months of the extended warranty.

questions.question: What does the warranty cover and not cover?
questions.answer:
Covers:

  • Manufacturing defects in the Fert Sensor and AT200 Monitor
  • Component failures under normal use conditions

Does not cover:

  • Power battery (consumable item)
  • Incorrect installation or installation outside the manual standards
  • Misuse, negligence, or lack of maintenance
  • Disassembly or repair by unauthorized personnel
  • External damage caused by the user
  • Natural wear of components

questions.question: How do I claim the warranty?
questions.answer: Contact Thunderstruck or J2M directly and provide the purchase invoice number. The warranty is a carry-in warranty — the product must be sent in for evaluation. Technicians do not visit the customer for installations or repairs.

questions.question: What does the Fert Sensor do?
questions.answer: It monitors the flow of fertilizer (granular input) through each line of the implement in real time. It detects blockages, interruptions, and irregular distribution — ensuring uniform application and preventing losses in the field.

questions.question: Which application types are compatible?
questions.answer: The system is compatible with Air Drill, Gravity, and Strip Till. For Air Drill, Gravity, and Strip Till, use the plastic clamp included with the product to secure it with the Hellermann Anti-UV strap.

questions.question: Which conduits are compatible?
questions.answer: Conduits from 25 mm to 100 mm in diameter. The recommended types are corrugated and hose. Rigid plastic conduits are not recommended.

questions.question: Does the sensor need cables or charging?
questions.answer: No. The Fert Sensor is completely wireless. It communicates via 915 MHz RF and is powered by a non-rechargeable lithium battery (3.6V – 4000mAh), with an approximate lifespan of 2,500 hours of use.

questions.question: The sensor stopped appearing on the monitor — what should I do?
questions.answer: Check whether the sensor is in power-saving mode (sleeping). Tap it lightly with a screwdriver to wake it up. If the issue persists, check the low battery warning on the monitor. If in doubt, contact technical support.

questions.question: How many sensors does the AT200 support?
questions.answer: The AT200 Monitor supports up to 120 sensors per monitor, communicating exclusively with FertiSystem sensors via 915 MHz RF.

questions.question: Does the monitor work on tractors without a cab?
questions.answer: Yes. The AT200 can be installed on tractors with or without a cab. See the installation recommendations in the manual for the correct setup in each case.

questions.question: What is the supply voltage?
questions.answer: The monitor operates on 12V DC, connected directly to the tractor battery. The 5 m power cable comes with the product, with an external 3A blade fuse.

questions.question: Is the monitor rain resistant?
questions.answer: Yes. The AT200 Monitor has IP54 protection — resistant to splashes of water from any direction. It is not intended for immersion or direct jets of water.

questions.question: How do I set the date and time?
questions.answer: Go to Menu → Clock and adjust day, month, year, hour, and minute using ▲▼. Confirm each field with ✓. The correct clock setting is important for alarm history.

questions.question: What does "MANEUVER" mean on the screen?
questions.answer: "MANEUVER" indicates that flow is interrupted across all or many lines at the same time — usually due to stopping the implement, turning at the end of the field, or refilling. It is not a fault alarm. When flow resumes, the monitor returns to "FLOW OK."

questions.question: What should I do when "FAULT" appears?
questions.answer: The indicated line has no flow while the others are normal. Check the corresponding line: there may be a blockage in the conduit or lack of material in that line’s metering unit.

questions.question: How do I use the alarm history?
questions.answer: Go to Menu → History and navigate with ▲▼. It records: monitor on/off events, maneuvers, start and end of planting, and each alarm with line number, date, and duration in seconds.

questions.question: Does the sensor with "LOW BATTERY" stop monitoring?
questions.answer: Not immediately. The alert appears in advance. The sensor continues operating for a period, but it is important to replace the battery so you do not lose monitoring on that line.

questions.question: Can I use it with any granular input?
questions.answer: Yes. The Fert Sensor is designed to monitor granular materials in general — fertilizers, cover crop seeds, and other inputs — in planting and cultivation operations for soybeans, corn, sugarcane, and more.

form-reminder.title: Ready to register?
form-reminder.subtitle: It takes less than 5 minutes — and doubles your coverage.
